reviews.review_text:
Did not expect to enjoy Survivor as much as I did. However, I have some complaints. The game has decent character progression, story progressed in an enjoyable and interesting way than its prequel, more combat variety with the introduction of multiple stances that can be switched mid fight and much more.

Survivor included everything its prequel was missing while also improving everything I enjoyed about it. This game actually felt like an open world with a drastic improvement in map design. Noticeably, Cal Kestis(main protagonist) feels stronger and more confident, from the beginning you can feel his growth as a Jedi throughout the years, he kept all skills learnt from the prequel while learning new ones throughout the journey which added depth to his character. I had low expectations regarding the story but character progression impressed me, the more I played the more engaged and curios I got. On a side note, as someone who enjoys Platinum trophies, this game had a fun journey with a well polished/connected world with shortcuts and fast travel points.

However, although Cal can fight in different stances, enemies felt a bit boring and repetitive due to lack of unique mechanics. Bosses felt underwhelming with me 1 shotting most of them, yes I was playing on normal difficulty but I expect some challenge through different mechanics since its labeled as a Souls-like game. Most bosses had 3-4 unique hits/abilities where most can be blocked, increasing difficulty meant that they hit harder and tighter block timing window, major disappointment Imo. Sadly this is one of the WORST optimized games in recent years, constant stuttering, frame drops, bugs, and crashing which was frustrating to say the very least.

The game delivered a semi Souls-like experience with STAR WARS theme that I thoroughly enjoyed, I can't wait for the upcoming sequel. I suggest playing Fallen Order before picking up Survivor, the story and character progression makes this game worthwhile, and hopefully EA will properly optimize the next game.

I want to enjoy this game but it runs like a paraplegic grandma who is also dead. Could only get to Jedha before the horrendous asset streaming and other bugs were too distracting too look past anymore

I really want to enjoy this game, as Jedi Fallen Order was a game I thoroughly enjoyed on my Xbox. This game has so much potential with new mechanics while keeping the same feel of the first game. However, it is hard to appreciate a game that crashes every 45 minutes. Unfortunately I don't think any fixes will be coming at this point, I personally can't pin down the reason as to why the crashes keep occurring.
Would be great if it didn't constantly crash. I cba to finish it when it runs so poorly and keeps crashing, will probably just watch a YouTube video since the story is good but that's about it.

i liked the first one better mainly due to the planets, cinematics, story and level design (it felt more souls-like)
this one's still really fun though, more open and exploration based, gameplay and graphics are better, story is good and has it's highlights but jedi fallen order had me more intrigued 
if jedi fallen order is a 10/10 (it is) this is an 8/10, hope they can do the best of both for the third game!
insane story, still needs optimization but 99% of the time i had no issue, just that 1% percent was annoying, defo worth a buy on sale as with any game

Another great game for the Jedi series with many new fighting styles and enemies to expand the soulslike experience of the first game. The amount of world exploration is also increased with the planet of Koboh being a particular highlight for its scale and frontier vibe. I spent a lot of hours just running around there enjoying the music and scenery, and finding collectibles. The saloon is very charming also! 

It is perhaps more easy to criticise this game though as compared to its predecessor. Most of the boss fights are simply too easy on Jedi Master difficulty. This difficulty felt perfect for the normal enemies (and the entirety of the first game) so I didn't want to change it. Although as many will probably tell you, [spoiler]Oggdo, double Oggdo and double Rancor[/spoiler] were brutal lol. Fighting ranged enemies also felt a lot worse than the first game. Deflecting massed blaster fire drains stamina like crazy and stuns you when you run out, while also being less trivial to do in this game while fighting melee enemies at the same time. And it's perfect blocks only to deflect the bolts back at the enemy so you have to just tank every subsequent hit with your stamina when facing enemies like the heavy weapons stormtrooper. Breaks the immersion into the Jedi experience a bit due to the frequency with which this becomes an issue. 

Performance was a big talking point with this game when it came out and my experience left me torn on this aspect. For most of my playtime I was able to run it at 1440p on all epic settings, with quality DLSS, at about 90-120 fps without RT (maybe 60-80 with). This was a perfectly good experience and left me pleasantly surprised, but weirdly I had a period of maybe 5-10 hours in the middle of my 75h playtime where it was just stuttering like crazy, frequently dropping from the above frame rates to 5-50fps for a few seconds. I could not work out why this was happening and when it went away I was back to normal. Tried some of the popular performance fix mods but they had no effect I could discern.

The story in this game is a bit more messy than Fallen Order's but I still enjoyed it. A lot of it revolves around the "High Republic" Star Wars lore which feels a bit forced to me, particularly in a game since this would have been a good opportunity to canonise some of the more entrenched Old Republic's lore which gamers may be more familiar with. The main twist of the game was quite interesting and involved some imperial factions being played off against each other. In this way it reminded me of the politicking we saw in Andor and real world history. Well written there I'd say. I was surprised they let Merrin and Cal have a romance also as I'd heard rumours they'd botched that.

Overall I'd recommend this game. If just performance issues are worrying you, I'd say from my experience you should just roll the dice.
Good story, Satisfying combat and creative worlds. Overall great game.

Cameron monaghan as Cal Kestis - goated. 
The story - super investing.
The executions/ finishers - Brutal.
The Clothing - Sick.

The game has a few minor issues, but this is THE best STAR WARS game for playing a jedi.
9,5/10

I've had quite a few performance issues so far and I'm only a few hours in.... however the story and gameplay are great!
Update: The characters and developments for cal have been absolutely peak and I'm loving the lightsaber styles. I do wish the cutscenes that include action didn't move at 10 fps but that's just me being picky
Final update after finishing: Absolute Cinema

it's a beautiful sequel with a lot of character development for Cal which I loved.
Graphics were not a huge leap ahead from the last game but still quite nice to look at. Lots of side things to do and items to collect that are actually mostly cosmetic so missing out on them gives little disadvantages in the gam. This was nice for me wanting to mainly follow the story line.
There are a lot of new things especially in terms of fighting mechanics that make the game quite fun and interesting.
It really makes me want a sequel!

There's a lot of performance issues that is kinda hard to recommend this unless you liked Fallen Order a lot and you're willing to take some stuttering and very occasional crashing and bugs as part of the experience.

This would be a top notch game otherwise. 

I was nevertheless hooked on this game because it did everything else so good:
Gameplay and Map -> Took the Fallen Order foundation to it's own league
Art, Story and Level Design --> I loved it. The Level design i think is too ambitious and they nailed it.

Conclusion:
It's a pretty good game with poor performance. My recommendations are
- Get this on a good discount
- Frame Gen is a MUST for the better experience; you'll have stutters and some crashings regardless but it will be way better.

Great game for star wars fans
Great sequel which builds on the gameplay of the first one and world building as well

The game is very hard. The bosses are very hard. The enemies are very hard. The music hits very hard. Mastering everything is very hard. I am very hard.

It is an amazing game though. 9.2/10
Great sequel that is better in almost every way, a lot of the issues with optimization have been ironed out and Cal Kestis is as great as ever.

playing on recommended requirements, game runs like ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, looks like ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. wouldnt recommend for non starwars fans and i still dont think EA would fix the performance issues and bugs, the game has been ou for more than 3 years

Honestly, I'm going to give a rare review where on this game i'm giving a downvote for general gameplay/atmosphere/story instead of the usual downvote for bugs/crashes.

I did get crashes that restarted my computer 4-5 times on Jedha but specifically, the actual story, characters and gameplay felt so mediocre 7/10. It's Okay but it wasn't great. The main character is great and his sidekick is great, that's where it ends.

The best boss you fight in the game, isn't as your character fighting, but as a mediocre side character. The actual story into the endgame is somewhat coherent until the darkside character just doesn't actually make a 50/50 decision that makes sense he makes like a 10/90 decision that's stiupid so there's no real ultimatum, the good guy decision is better and the only logical decision and it ruins the story. The bad guy sacrifices their life for literally no reason.

Honestly Star Wars in general has been garbage outside of half of Mandalorian and most of Andor in the past few years. This just follows that. The planets aren't great, the exploration depends heavily on doing the story first and I just wanted the game to be over which is rare for me. 

The first game is much better.

So overall the game is not terrible and does have some really great moments.  But the gameplay feels like constant busy work that is more annoying than fun.  Cal is in the prime of his life yet he can't run up a sloped hill that a 70 year old person could walk up in game.  Instead you have to become batman and pull out your utility belt to leap around different structures to merely get around an incline hill that a person in a wheel chair could roll up.  The game feels like a mountain climbing simulator.  You spend 75% of your game time falling off cliffs, leaping to your death, or climbing around obstacles to get from point A to god knows where because the mapping system is harder to read than an old generic flat map.  I would say you could chop the hours down in this game from 25 that I played to like 10 without all the travel time of jumping, climbing, and not being able to get your GPS to work correctly.  The interaction and growth between characters simply is not there because the game doesn't explore personal relationships between characters.  Cal and Merrin care for each other but I couldn't really tell you why.  The best character in the game is actually Cere Junda.  The character has a driving purpose and the voice actor actually does a good job and playing as her is way more fun than playing as Cal.  To be honest, Cal is just not really a good main character for the franchise.  There just isn't much to him to actually like other than he is trying to do the right thing.  Why Cal is so driven to hunt down Dagan Gera to prevent him from reaching Tanalorr really doesn't make much sense as the whole sequence of events do not really have much to do with Cal as they occurred very long time ago.  Bode is a greatly designed character but making him the main boss on Tanalorr and somehow having him even more powerful than Dagan Gera?  Makes no sense.  You finally reach Tanalorr only to spend 10 minutes walking around before you ultimately face off against bode.  Serious build up to a secretly hidden planet to explore it for 10 minutes.

Took me a couple of tries to get through this game. The intro and later sections are pretty solid but some chunks in the middle felt more sluggish and I lost interest. The gameplay has some solid souls-lite with some fun boss fights. I was having some framerate issues and entering loading spots sometimes tanked performance. Overall, Jedi Survivor is solid, and if you're really into Star Wars, will scratch that itch.

It honestly just feels hollow, everything seems to be there to fit a gameplay purpose and not because they wanted to craft a believable world. It kind of feels like newer Assassin's Creed games. The developers seem to have been given puzzle pieces of what a game "must contain" and the game is the result of these randomly being tossed together. It feels soulless. 

Besides that, you are fighting 10% of the time and 90% you are climbing. You are literally always climbing, the whole world seems to be a parcour. On top of that the movement mechanics just aren't as refined as they should be given that climbing is such a big part of the game.

And do not even get me started on the enemy design. I like playing on the harder difficulties and this game just makes your time miserable. Especially those frogs are just horrible. So many abilities are just oneshots regardless of how much you invest into your max HP. To top this off, the designers thought having two of these would be a great idea. Leading you to dodge on ability and getting into unavoidable overlaps where your dodges end with you being hit by the other frog's oneshot. There is no fun in not being able to (or barely being able to) influence the outcome. The other "story" bosses are way better, almost too easy again because they do not oneshot you and their abilities are way more forgiving. It really seems as the frogs were just thrown in as a filler without giving them any thought.

Comparing the boss design in this game to a fromsoftware game really makes you wonder how drastic the difference is. We are talking about EA who are trying to get 70 bucks from us for this game. It really is absurd.

I got another one: sometimes (often in cutscenes) the textures are not loading and you are mostly looking at flat surfaces in the world or even characters. Once again, comparing the performance to say Crimson Desert just makes EA look like fools who aren't trying. But they still charge us like they produced a banger.

Ye, I really just can't recommend this. Even though I think I liked the previous game. Maybe I would be as annoyed if I played it again. Who knows. But this just feels ♥♥♥♥, really can't recommend buying this.
Gameplay is intriguing, graphics are great, game mechanics and options are fun. Game also crashes every 20 minutes and couldn't be finished because of crashes :(
not a fan of the dialog, or any of the characters (except bd), runs horribly. fun gameplay, just not my thing.

Performance issues aside, I had a great time with this game, I enjoyed the story much more than the first game, loved the characters and the locations, traversal tech and combat felt better. Here's hoping we've got one more Cal Kestis adventure on the way to end this story.
How is a game that's been out this long such a buggy mess? Screen chop, loading zone lag, and more while I'm running a 5060 ti. Doesn't make sense.

A step up from Fallen Order in terms of gameplay and exploration. Really love the puzzles and similarities to metroidvanias. The characters really shine in this game, however I found the story to be one of the weaker aspects of the game.

PC performance is absolutely a talking point as it may differ for everyone and can be a deal breaker for some depending on their set up. I'm running a 7900XTX and R5 5600 on Arch Linux. With graphics settings turned up to Epic quality and no FSR, I was averaging ~85 FPS. Certain areas and boss encounters had my FPS dipping down to the 30s, but stutters were pretty infrequent for me. I see a lot of variation in how well this game runs, so I'm boiling it down to hardware. It's certainly not optimized and I'm running a GPU that can brute force frames, you may not. If the game interests you (and I do highly recommend it if you enjoy Star Wars and even moreso if you enjoyed the first game), I might recommend picking it up on a console unfortunately.

I know most of the complaints about Jedi Survivor are related to performance, but I personally didn’t encounter a single crash or bug. Aside from that, the game was an absolute blast. The graphics are incredible, the gameplay is engaging, and exploration is rewarding. The challenges strike a nice balance and not too difficult, but still satisfying to overcome. Going for 100% completion and all the achievements was a lot of fun. I highly recommend it, especially if you can pick it up on a deep sale.

Everytime I was lost and confused where to go, I had to pull up Youtube 💀
The game was still alright tho but it was also unoptimized, playable but unoptimized.

STAR WARS Jedi: Survivor takes everything that worked in Fallen Order and expands it into a much larger and more ambitious experience. Bigger environments, more combat styles, more exploration, more progression systems, and a story that feels significantly more emotional and mature than the first game.

The strongest aspect of the game is easily its narrative and atmosphere. The story evolves naturally from Fallen Order and manages to keep that same sense of tension, betrayal, fear, and emotional weight that made the previous game memorable. Cal’s journey feels more personal this time, and the ending leaves a strong setup for the future of the franchise.

Visually, the game is beautiful. The environments, lighting, facial animations, and overall presentation are a clear improvement over the first game. Some moments genuinely feel cinematic in the best possible way, especially during larger story sequences and character interactions.

However, the technical state of the game continues to be its biggest problem. Even years after launch, performance issues are still very noticeable. Stutters, unstable frametimes, texture pop-in, visual bugs, lighting glitches, geometry issues, and inconsistent optimization remain present throughout the experience. Some areas perform normally, while others suddenly suffer from heavy FPS drops for no clear reason. This becomes especially frustrating during exploration or combat-heavy moments.

The combat system also feels like it hasn’t evolved as much as it should have. There is more variety now, with different stances and expanded skill trees, but the core structure still relies too heavily on dodge, parry, animation timing, and Souls-like mechanics. Personally, I think Star Wars should lean more into dynamic Jedi combat rather than trying so hard to imitate a Souls formula.

The Force itself feels too limited during gameplay. In the movies and series, Jedi constantly manipulate the environment around them, but here most interactions feel scripted or restricted to specific moments instead of being part of a more dynamic combat system. There’s huge potential for environmental interaction that still hasn’t been fully explored.

Exploration is another mixed aspect. The open-area structure is much larger this time, which is good for immersion and scale, but traversal eventually becomes repetitive. The game introduces mounts and creatures for movement, but there’s still a feeling that the world was not fully designed around fluid traversal systems. Combined with the large amount of collectibles scattered around the map, backtracking can sometimes become tiring.

Despite all of these criticisms, the game still succeeds at delivering a genuinely strong Star Wars experience. The story is engaging, the characters are memorable, the atmosphere is excellent, and the overall presentation carries the experience through many of its flaws.

In the end, Jedi Survivor feels like a sequel that aimed much higher than Fallen Order. It doesn’t fully succeed in every aspect, especially technically, but the ambition is clearly there. More importantly, it leaves enough emotional impact and narrative potential to make me genuinely excited for the next game.

Score: 8/10

(6) Fine (7) Good (8) Very Good (9) Great (10) Master Piece

Waited in the hopes this game would be fixed overtime.
Pretty sure we are like 5 years on & the game stutters more than my cousin with Tourettes.

Garbage.

game kinda easy unless youre on the hardest difficulty, but then it stops being fun. my main issue was solved with ng+ being that lightsabers deal a fun amount of damage not a realistic amount of damage. Took almost exactly 60 hours to 99% the game, all I missed was something that required me to run around and grab seeds so I could unlock the map to find said seeds.

combat good, sandbox good, climbing boring and game kinda easy but it's an EA slop game i don't expect pure gold.
7.5/10
this game is good, but it crashes too ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ much. fix the game please.

It was an unforgettable journey through my beloved universe. After watching all the movies for the first time, I realized that I didn't have enough movies and needed more Star Wars content, so it led me to the first part of Fallen Order, which I generally liked, but not enthusiastically. And when I found out that there was a second one, I ran to pass it. And what was my surprise that the second part turned out to be MUCH better, literally everything was improved here - customization, new gameplay features were added, gardening, collecting fish (this is important), additional quests, EVERYTHING was improved. I would also like to mention the bosses, as the battles with them are super interesting, they are all DIFFERENT, with their cool chips. Unfortunately, it is also necessary to note the optimization of the game, and endless bugs, for some reason it was very difficult for me, the game crashed, there were a lot of bugs, the textures loaded poorly, BUT even this did not spoil my experience from passing.

Well, and most importantly, the plot. I deeply felt for the main character, I empathized with him throughout the game from beginning to end, thank you to the developers for giving my beloved universe such a well-developed character with his injuries from the effects of the Empire, thank you for revealing what it was like to be a Jedi after everything that happened, thank you for touching on the topic of Kal's inner struggle with the dark side, thank you that you weave everything into lore so harmoniously. At the end, I burst into tears, I want to believe that Kal will be able to cope with all the difficulties that fell on his difficult path as a Jedi, I believe......may the force be with him.

No matter what pc specs you have the game will run like ♥♥♥♥ and will crash every 2 seconds. Trust me save your money and don't buy the game until Respawn and EA can make the game run better (Which is probably never, because both studios don't give 2 about the people that play their games).

When this first came out on PC, I was curious but skipped it for the brutal performance issues. Three years and nine patches later, I bought it dirt cheap on sale, and was pleasantly surprised because I'm having an absolute blast with it. No performance issues or stuttering whatsoever on my old rig (i7-7700K / GTX 2080 + 8 GB / 32 GB RAM), but a game that's bigger, better, and a lot more fun and versatile than its predecessor, with more powers, stances, mounts, cosmetics and what-not, all wrapped up in a neat blockbuster production.

Bottom Line, this actually works as intended now, as it plays and feels like it's worth the raving reviews from back then. For me, its easily a 9/10, and likely the best SP Star Wars game I've played since Jedi: Outcast.
This is a very good game plagued by an initial rocky launch. Runs great on my PC no crashes or performance issues in 16 hours.
would probably be a cool game and have a cool story if you can get past Cal's inability to dodge more than 2ft like he just got a double leg transplant and had them put on backwards.

Metroidvania was replaced with a bland ubisoft open world and half of the imputes in combat refuse to work right. On top of that the game crashes a lot and several of the menus always drop frames down into the 20s, in spite of the gameplay always in the 60s. Some times load times with just freeze for 3-4 minutes, the only redeeming qualities are the characters and story.
game crashes, performance is trash, game stops accepting controller input mid playthrough

5hr in and what can i say its an EA game. I enjoyed the first one better somehow and i will say the second just feels like they do not give a ♥♥♥♥♥ about combat aside from aesthetics and "looking cool." dont get me wrong i like engaging difficult combat. but so many glitchy, messed up moments happen in this washed up painted ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that i feel like i shld do anything at all.

The combat system has not been fixed since the first game, it was bad, it still is bad. The story exists. The main antagonist is flat and boring. Performance is mediocre.

Exploration and movement are fun though.

i think its pretty crappy that i cannot get the hermit outfit love the game PO'ed that i cannot get the hermit outfit dunno why they would sell stuff for the first game and not do it with this. and i dont know how to mod so, yeah ,booooo
Great story and gameplya, minus optimization

My love‑hate relationship with this game was really evident.

Combat is fluid and improved from Fallen Order. Every boss battle feels like an epic duel. The storyline adds real emotional weight to Cal’s journey, while the world design is diverse and rewarding to explore. Traversal is fun and intuitive—probably one of the best I’ve experienced. You can double jump, grapple, wall run, ride BD‑1 on ziplines, use mounts, and air dash (which even lets you pass through laser gates), all of which are incredibly satisfying.

The only downside is that it’s held back by occasional performance hiccups and constant crashes. Probably the only game that I played that will crash everytime I played it.

Overall, it’s a thrilling Star Wars experience that nails gameplay, story, and atmosphere.
very fun story game great combat and fun puzzels and heres a tip always be awer of your suroundings
actually i was confused about which choice should i choose because i thought this game still have a bug despite it has a good gameplay experience

Great Star wars Adventure game

Pros > 
-fight are better than the previous game
-Story is OK

Cons>
-Not as much planet
-Exploration is kinda Off

good overall game and had a lot of fun. but the reason why I leave a bad review is because the optimization is horrendous on this game. the stability patches they made have barely put a dent into performance. not to mention the game's TAA implementation is horrible even on the EPIC settings which causes the game to look very blurry. If you want to play this game with a stable framerate and sharp picture you need to download various mods for it. This is a prime example of a badly optimized game

Nearly 100% the game on console, purchased on PC with the intentions of truly finishing 100% at some point but haven't had the chance to put tons of hours into it yet, but I wanted to share my general thoughts on the game overall while also touching on my PC performance experience so far.
TLDR, the game is a very solid sequel with a lot of content, good combat, a great story, and your mileage may vary on performance. Most definitely worth it on any sale, and as long as you like these games and get good performance it'll probably rank high on your all time list for Star Wars games.

By far one of the best Star Wars stories ever told. Cal Kestis is one of the most well realized and compelling characters we've ever gotten in Star Wars and this game really helps to build on his arc in a meaningful way. I like the direction taken with the rest of the returning characters from Fallen Order, while not a perfect continuation for all of them, I was satisfied enough with where they ended up by the end of the story and am eager to see what I can only assume will be the end of their stories in the third game.

Cal maintains pretty much every ability learned through the first game and just adds to his kit here, which helps this game feel less restrictive right from the beginning, and provides a lot of freedom of expression in combat from the very start. The lightsaber and BD customization is back and I would argue better than the first game just based on my personal taste, however we do lose Mantis colorways in exchange for blaster customization. Cal also gets a pretty substantial style upgrade here, there's definitely some questionable choices to be found, but on the whole, this is an improvement from the options we got in the first game which is basically just PONCHO. I actually agree with Cameron though and would've liked to have more ponchos but beggars can't be choosers I suppose. 

My problems with this game are actually fairly tame and actually have a lot to do with the pacing of levels and character writing. This game has the issue of dragging out areas for whatever reason and it's always areas that are somewhat frustrating to navigate. This really only happens maybe 3-4 times, but it's enough that on a second playthrough, you find yourself less interested in finding everything on your initial run through an area and instead just rushing through them as fast as possible. (although, this game did only have 1 or 2 puzzles that I would consider to be straight up poorly designed garbage with one of them being entirely optional which is very much appreciated) 
My second problem is the character writing but only for the villains of which there are more than I was expecting. We actually see a surprisingly small amount of these main villains however, which I know isn't super uncommon and was something that certainly also happened in the first game as well, but it just feels like there was a lot left on the table and none of the resolutions felt like they were long in the tooth nor did they have much payoff for me personally. I always got to the end of a section with a villain and just kind of ended up going "huh, that was it I guess, interesting." Only two of the boss fights felt like they had any emotional weight but damn did they hit hard. I think the game ultimately sticks the landing with a VERY strong final act, there's just some weaker small sections sprinkled throughout that don't ruin the game by any means, I think I'm really just nitpicking after having played through some of the sections more than once. 

Performance is not an issue that I've ever had with this game personally but it is something I would be cautious of. I'm getting a locked 60fps at 4k max settings using DLSS for anti aliasing (ray tracing turned off because it does next to nothing here) and I rarely see dips below maybe 55 in especially demanding situations, though again, your mileage may vary here. Bugs are nothing unplayable but they're definitely here. You will unfortunately find yourself jumping and getting caught on pieces of terrain and getting stuck until the game registers you as falling for too long, as well as Cal just not grabbing things that he's actively touching, but aside from these, I never really had anything egregious. 

Overall, I'd say this is a strong 8-light 9/10 as long as it runs well for you and is definitely worth it especially on sale. There's no shortage of content and I'd say, if you give the game time to show you what it's got, you could EASILY get 60ish hours out of a playthrough if you do the side quests and bounty hunting alongside the main story

Dang and I thought the first game was good this one builds very well from Jedi Fallen Order with another amazing story. Also the customization in this game compared to its predecessor is an added bonus. Finally I personally didn't run into any performance issues.

Surprisingly for EA, this is a good game with a good story.
It improves upon the things that were subpar in Jedi: Fallen Order (biggest change being the introduction of fast travel system to make backtracking more bearable). That said, there are still times where the minimap is barely readable due to overlapping verticality and some force rifts / trials were frustrating BS with enemies one-shotting you [spoiler] 2x Ogdo Bogdo and 2x Rancor [/spoiler]. I'm also not the biggest fan of backtracking for mere cosmetics and collectibles.
On the technical side, game ran fine, with minor FPS drops here and there and crashed only once during the entire playthrough.

The video game *Star Wars Jedi: Survivor* is one of the most impressive action-adventure titles released in recent years. Developed by Respawn Entertainment and published by Electronic Arts, the game serves as the sequel to *Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order*. It follows the journey of Cal Kestis, a Jedi Knight struggling to survive in a galaxy controlled by the Galactic Empire. Through its engaging story, exciting combat, stunning visuals, and meaningful character development, *Jedi: Survivor* successfully delivers an unforgettable experience for both Star Wars fans and general gamers alike. Although the game has a few minor flaws, it stands out as a remarkable achievement in modern gaming.

One of the strongest aspects of *Jedi: Survivor* is its story. The narrative takes place several years after the events of *Fallen Order*, showing a more experienced and mature Cal Kestis. Unlike many action games that focus solely on combat, *Jedi: Survivor* explores themes of hope, sacrifice, friendship, and perseverance. Cal faces numerous challenges throughout his journey, including difficult choices that test his beliefs and commitment to the Jedi way. The story feels personal and emotional, allowing players to become invested in the characters and their struggles. The writing is strong, and the pacing keeps players interested from beginning to end.

Another reason why *Jedi: Survivor* is so good is its excellent character development. Cal Kestis grows significantly throughout the game, becoming a more complex and relatable protagonist. Supporting characters such as Merrin, Greez, and Cere Junda also receive meaningful development. Their interactions feel natural and believable, making the crew's relationships one of the highlights of the experience. The game does not rely solely on action to engage players; instead, it builds emotional connections that make important story moments even more impactful.

The combat system is another major strength of the game. Building upon the mechanics introduced in *Fallen Order*, *Jedi: Survivor* offers a wider variety of lightsaber stances and combat options. Players can choose between different fighting styles depending on their preferences. Whether using a traditional single-bladed lightsaber, dual wielding, employing a crossguard stance, or combining lightsaber combat with a blaster, the game provides flexibility and depth. Each stance feels unique and encourages experimentation. The combat is challenging but rewarding, requiring players to learn enemy patterns, time their attacks carefully, and use Force abilities strategically.

Exploration is also significantly improved in *Jedi: Survivor*. The game's environments are larger, more detailed, and more rewarding to explore than those of its predecessor. Players can discover hidden treasures, optional bosses, collectibles, and side quests throughout the galaxy. The sense of adventure is enhanced by the variety of planets and locations available. Each area has its own identity, architecture, and atmosphere, helping the galaxy feel alive and immersive. Exploration rarely feels like a chore because players are consistently rewarded for their curiosity.

Visually, *Jedi: Survivor* is an impressive achievement. The environments are beautifully designed, featuring detailed landscapes, vibrant lighting, and cinematic presentation. From ancient ruins to bustling settlements, every location showcases a high level of artistic craftsmanship. Character models are highly detailed, and facial animations help convey emotions effectively during story scenes. Combined with high-quality visual effects, lightsaber combat appears dynamic and spectacular. The game succeeds in capturing the look and feel of the Star Wars universe in a way that few titles have managed before.

The audio design deserves praise as well. The soundtrack perfectly complements the game's emotional and action-packed moments. Inspired by the iconic musical style of the Star Wars franchise, the music helps create an authentic atmosphere. Sound effects are equally impressive, from the hum of lightsabers to the roar of spacecraft engines. Voice acting throughout the game is consistently strong, with the cast delivering believable performances that bring the characters to life.

Another feature that contributes to the game's success is its customization system. Players have numerous options to personalize Cal's appearance, lightsaber design, and even the appearance of BD-1. This level of customization allows players to express their creativity and develop a stronger connection with their character. While customization does not directly affect gameplay, it adds an enjoyable layer of personalization that many players appreciate.

The game also excels at balancing action and storytelling. Some games focus heavily on one aspect at the expense of the other, but *Jedi: Survivor* manages to provide exciting gameplay while maintaining a compelling narrative. Players are constantly switching between exploration, combat, puzzle-solving, and story progression, preventing the experience from becoming repetitive. This variety helps maintain engagement throughout the lengthy campaign.

Of course, no game is perfect. One criticism some players have mentioned involves technical issues present at launch, particularly performance problems on certain platforms. While many of these issues have been improved through updates, they affected some players' initial impressions. Additionally, there is the bd 1 goo. Despite being a relatively small part of the overall experience, the bd 1 goo can occasionally be frustrating or less enjoyable compared to the game's strongest features. However, this issue is minor when compared to the numerous strengths found throughout the game.

In conclusion, *Star Wars Jedi: Survivor* is an outstanding action-adventure game that successfully builds upon the foundation established by its predecessor. Its engaging story, memorable characters, deep combat system, rewarding exploration, impressive visuals, and excellent audio design combine to create a truly exceptional experience. While minor issues such as technical performance concerns and the bd 1 goo prevent it from being absolutely flawless, these shortcomings do little to diminish the game's overall quality. For Star Wars fans and action-adventure enthusiasts alike, *Jedi: Survivor* represents one of the best gaming experiences available and serves as a shining example of what modern single-player games can achieve.

I enjoyed this one a lot more than the previous game. It fixed most of the things that annoyed me in the first one, especially having to revisit planets over and over again and constantly getting lost. Getting lost hasn’t completely disappeared, but it’s much less frustrating compared to the first game.

I expected the game to do the usual sequel thing where you lose all your abilities and start from scratch, but thankfully that wasn’t the case. It keeps most of the powers and skills you earned by the end of the first game while adding new abilities as the story progresses, which made the progression feel much more natural.

The story isn’t anything amazing, but it does its job. The real strength of the game is how much it improves the gameplay and exploration compared to its predecessor.

As for the optimization, by 2026 it’s in a much better state than what I had heard or read about. There are still some issues—I had three crashes during my playthrough—but other than that I didn’t run into any major problems that affected my experience.

[h1]Star Wars Jedi: Survivor[/h1]

---

## ⚠️ Spoiler Warning ##

This review contains story spoilers. I will also mention rumours about Jedi 3, so take those with a grain of salt.

---

Star Wars Jedi Survivor is one of the best ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ games I've played in the last few years. The game is ridiculously good, the combat is fun, the music is magnificent, and I'm still fan-girling from the fact that The HU is just... ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ there. Like, what??

I played for 30 hours, which is give or take enough to see almost everything you can do, plus the main quest. I unfortunately didn't want to bother with the bounties, for example, which is definitely my favourite side activity other than petting the bogling (yes, you can pet the boglings). Though I should probably return to it before Jedi 3 comes out, which I'm really ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ excited about considering how good the story is.

---

[h2]Combat[/h2]

Fluid motions, very easy to learn, extremely satisfying, but can be hard, especially on harder difficulties. I did play on normal, but I did struggle with some bosses like:

* [spoiler]Darth Vader[/spoiler]
* Spawn of Oggdo (still pissed about this one, it's such a piece of ♥♥♥♥ boss fight)

The fighting feels good, like really good. I mostly used the normal stance with a single blade, just made me feel better, plus I was already used to it from Fallen Order. It's a classical stance in my opinion and makes you feel like a Jedi.

The other stances are fun as well, though I didn't use them as much, especially the double-bladed stance. I didn't see a reason to, other than once or twice in the beginning because the game forced you to.

The two handed stance is really solid, though the way Cal uses them, the animations in particular looked weird, but not that big of an issue.

The blaster stance was such a nice addition aaaand a bit of a disappointment. When I saw the cover art, I was imagining that you'd be able to use it like in Red Dead, where you have the option to fight with the blaster alone, but nope, it's a mix between a lightsaber and a blaster. It's fine, but I still feel like if you could've used the blaster as a single weapon rather than a new stance with the blaster, it would've been better.

The last stance, Crossguard, I barely used. The lightsaber looked cool because its mechanism changed physically, it's literally Kylo Ren's lightsaber, which is one of the best looking lightsabers along with Count Dooku's.

The issue is it's sooo ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ slow. As a mechanic I get it, you want something that hits harder but slower, makes sense. Every game that has melee weapons does that. Not every game has ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lightsabers though. They are supposed to be fast and light, not this. It feels useless, especially if you're used to the other stances, plus you get it a bit too late in the game, and it makes you wonder why you would spend points and time upgrading it.

---

[h2]The World(s)[/h2]

One of my favourite things about Star Wars is travelling in space and seeing new worlds, races, fauna and flora. They nailed this, even in Fallen Order.

[h3]Koboh[/h3]

The vibe of this planet is amazing. Old western style in Star Wars, I love it. The town, the saloon, the surrounding area and different biomes are all put together really well. A lot of people say it's extremely laggy, and the game did crash, but luckily I didn't have any issues. I did have an issue with that, when i was choosing which planet to go in, but i wouldn't say it impacted my experience much, my game did crash once, while exiting it as well, which is funny to me, but yeah.

---

[h3]Jedha[/h3]

Beautiful planet again. Love the desert vibes, love the fauna and flora. I'm not gonna talk much because there is none. I didn't explore it as much, but I very much like it and recommend strolling around it.

Also has my favourite cutscenes in the game, and the first time meeting (in this game) the best character in the whole game, Merrin of course :3

---

[h3]Coruscant[/h3]

Unfortunately I can't talk much about it because there is nothing to do there. You start in the capital, you're there once, and you don't really go back unless you want collectibles, which is disappointing CONSIDERING IT'S THE ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ CAPITAL OF THE EMPIRE. Still cool though.

---

[h3]Shattered Moon (Koboh)[/h3]

I see the appeal. Beautiful scenery, but again, not much to do there. Other then story related quests and bunch of collectable.

---

[h3]Nova Garon[/h3]

I don't wanna talk much about it, but you can go there any time. Seeing Cal there is extremely cool. I'll talk more about it in the story section. Amazing place visually.

---

[h3]Tanalorr[/h3]

It's beautiful, it's pretty. The boss fight was underwhelming, and there's not much to do. The whole point of the game was to get there, just to be a boring place... sad.

---

[h2]THE STORY[/h2]

[spoiler]From the very beginning, starting in the capital, Cal being caught, thinking it's over, only to find out it was all a hoax to capture a senator, to being confused why Bode sounds so familiar, then hearing him say a very specific line in a very specific voice I had heard once before in Red Dead 2 as Charles, got me even more invested.

All of this made me want to continue the story more and more, which was a great decision.

From Coruscant to Koboh, seeing Greez again, finding old Republic stuff, droids and even a "Jedi", was really cool. The way it unfolds is simple: you go to fix your ship and get pulled into something much bigger. I like it. It works really well. Sometimes simplicity is better than overwhelming the player.

Then going to Jedha, seeing Merrin, Cere, and Master Cordova... I don't want to talk too much about it. I want people to experience it themselves if they want a good Star Wars story.[/spoiler]

---

[h2]Jedi 3 (Rumours / Speculation)[/h2]

VICE reported that the game leaked: https://www.vice.com/en/article/first-star-wars-jedi-3-details-reportedly-revealed/

I also saw a YouTube video from WesNemo, a YouTube short a month ago, and a video by "Bombastic" two months ago. I haven't seen proper official news, but I hope Jedi 3 comes out in the next 2-3 years.

I want to see what Cal does next. I want to see him struggle with the dark side. I want Merrin to react realistically if he falls. I want redemption or consequences that actually matter. Maybe even a white lightsaber, in-universe reason or not.

I want Cal to feel human in Star Wars. To struggle. To panic. To lose everything because he loses his path.

The first real step toward that was probably using a blaster, something he himself said he shouldn't do.

Everything is building perfectly, I just hope Respawn doesn't lose the thread. What they've built is genuinely beautiful, and it deserves a strong conclusion.

Does Cal keep Merrin? Do we get a proper moment between them? Or does he become something worse...
Awesome star wars game, has some negatives but the story and light saber combat make up for everything hehe

It's sad that the console experience of this game is infinitely superior to the PC version. Mods can help with crashing but you'll still see heavy stuttering. The game runs completely fine in the first area, and then once you get to the second planet it's crash city. Mods can help with crashing but you'll still see heavy stuttering. I did, at least. It's just genuinely not fun to play with performance this bad.

If you have a Series X or PS5, I would play it there. Don't bother with this version of the game.

Super fun game but I feel like I can only enjoy it on my days off from work. Being restricted to specific save points feels really outdated in today's modern videogame landscape. I only have a few hours of freedom every workday to play games and I have to budget my time wisely, because I could potentially lose a half hour of progress when I need to close the game and go to work. Other than that, it's a really great game. I highly recommend it.

Looking forward to the third instalment.

The game is very good and the story and writing especially so. The combat makes really great use of the different stances you can use. The one major gripe that takes this game from being my favorite game of all time to just a good game is it's performance. Yeah I don't have the greatest pc but the last game I could run max settings at 60 fps, this game however I have to run all settings on low and enable FSR to get past 30 fps. I feel if they left it in the oven for another 6 months to find ways of optimizing the experience it would have been a lot better. I hope they don't make the same mistake with the next game this franchise has such an amazing story to tell and it stings to see it's potential ruined by the performance. Overall I give this game an 9.5/10 not considering the performance but since the performance can make the game downright unplayable with crashes I cant give it anything higher than an 8/10. 

P.S: My hours say 63 hours but my game file says a little less than 50 hours (I cant remember correctly) meaning I've lost somewhere around 15 hours of time due to crashes resetting my progress just to give you an idea of how often the crashes were.

This game was awesome.  Story was interesting from start to finish.  Character building was also great.  You become invested in each member of the story on both sides.  Lots of twists,  turns, and surprises along the way.  The movement and combat, use of of force powers, use of BD-1, and environment are all really well done.  Good amount of collectibles/cosmetics for you to find, encouraging you to explore more of the world.  Lots oof puzzles that are interesting and fun to figure out.  Visuals are incredible.  Definitely the best Jedi based game I've ever played.  The first game to beat out KOTOR imho.  I also enjoyed Fallen Order, and this one tops that by a mile...

I give this game a thumbs up mainly because it taught me that I'm not really a fan of this style of game. This is an enjoyable game. If you liked the first one you will definitely like this one too since its a more open world and deep explorable than the first. However one thing that messes up with the experience its the bad monitization of the game. There are several bugs and frame drops during the game. It frustrating to see a good game being dragged down by things like this. Another thing i was expecting is more options with the lightsaber since you can only customize it. The new stances though really make up for that since there are more play styles for us to choose. Lightsaber sound effects and battles are really satisfying i have to say. Hope the next game has more options with the weapons and builds you can do.

Together with Jedi Fallen Order these were the first Swordplay,story-driven games I played. In terms of story, this is for me definitely up there with a very good Star-Wars movie and super innovative: It never fells like you would just put a existing movie into a story, but discover something entirely new. And I would definitely recommend playing Fallen Order first, I bought it at a discount but think its worth the full price.


There is definitely a lot of space for improvement in terms of the fighting mechanics, especially when there are multiple enemies or bosses that just permanently spam unblockable attacks, that really is just annoying. I would also like a option to entirely disable replay of cinematics on bosses, as watching the same cinematic when repeating a boss 20 times makes the whole thing feel kind of cheap. Some times even the story mode difficulty get you hung up on bosses, when you really just wanna enjoy the story. Apart from that, I think there definitely is some fun in fighting and i loved the dual wielded stance.

Crashes, bug, poor optimization, and poorer design. This game wishes it could resemble some of the genius design of soulslike and others, but it just falls flat. Cal is clunky and slow, enemies (especially bosses) are incredibly overtuned and have ridiculous mechanics. and the game is so poorly optimized for PC. I love Star Wars, but this is just such a bad experience.

by some miracle, it actually runs fine on my PC, so that's out of the way.

gameplay is so much better than Fallen Order, you keep all your abilities and just tack on new ones, its refreshing. no Metroid suit reset; you're a very powerful force user by the end of the game. too many collectibles though, [spoiler]i already completed the garden and there's still 100 seeds to find.[/spoiler]

story-wise, its a bit weaker than Fallen Order, only because you're expected to roam around a bit between each mission but its never hinted to you, so adjacent chapters have somewhat jarring character transitions based on time you never spent with them. Also, Merrin needs some better npc dialogue, her cutscenes tell a completely different story than her in-game conversations do, [spoiler]and honestly the voice actress is pretty wooden[/spoiler]. I thought Bode was a great character by contrast, and Greez is still an amazing uncle. You don't really see Vader in this game, but you hear about him running his own operation somewhat against yours, it's a cool way to keep him in.

the performance is a complete gamble so i don't recommend, and the story is a bit weak in cutscene-movie form, so i think watching a playthrough is probably your best bet. Greezy money, baby.
